document.docAuthor: Don E. Wilson, Russell A. Mittermeier & Thomas E. Lacher, Jr
document.docOrigin: Handbook of the Mammals of the World – Volume 7 Rodents II, Barcelona: Lynx Edicions

[MISSING], 652. [MISSING], 653. [MISSING], 654. [MISSING], 655. [MISSING], 656. [MISSING], 657. [MISSING], 659. Brown Rat (Rattus norvegicus), 660. Roof Rat (Rattus rattus), 661. [MISSING], 662. [MISSING], 664. Himalayan Rat (Rattus pyctoris), 665. Ranjini’s Rat (Rattus ranjiniae), 666. Sahyadris Forest Rat (Rattus satarae), 667. Andaman Archipelago Rat (Rattus stoicus), 669. Pacific Rat (Rattus exulans), 670. Indochinese Forest Rat (Rattus andamanensis), 672. Losea Rat (Rattus losea), 673. White-footed Indochinese Rat (Rattus nitidus), 674. Osgood’s Vietnamese Rat (Rattus osgoodi), 675. Little Indochinese Field Rat (Rattus sakeratensis), 676. Burnished Enggano Rat (Rattus adustus), 677. Kinabalu Rat (Rattus baluensis), 678. Aceh Rat (Rattus blangorum), 679. Enggano Island Rat (Rattus enganus), 680. Hoogerwert’s Sumatran Rat (Rattus hoogerwerfi), 682. Mentawai Archipelago Rat (Rattus lugens), 683. Gag Island Rat (Rattus nikenu), 684. Simalur Archipelago Rat (Rattus simalurensis), 685. Malaysian Field Rat (Rattus tiomanicus), 686. South-western Xanthurus Rat (Rattus bontanus), 687. Hoffmann’s Sulawesi Rat (Rattus hoffmanni), 688. Koopman' ’ s Peleng Island Rat (Rattus koopmani), 689. Marmoset Xanthurus Rat (Rattus marmosurus), 690. Lampobatang Sulawesi Rat (Rattus mollicomulus)

subSubSection.paragraph: French: Rat de Simeulue / German: Simalur-Ratte / Spanish: Rata de Simalur

paragraph.vernacularName:
Simalur Rat
Taxonomy.
Mus simalurensis G. S. Miller, 1903
,

subSubSection.paragraph: Phylogenetic relationship of R. simalurensis in Rattus is currently unknown, although it may be related to R.tiomanicus , of which it may be anisland variant of other Sundaic Island rats ( R. burrus , R. adustus , and R. lugens ) based on morphology. Two subspecies have been recognized in the past. Monotypic.

caption.paragraph: Distribution. Simalur, Siumat, Lasia, and Babi Is, off NW Sumatra.

subSubSection.paragraph: Descriptive notes. Head-body 200-213 mm,tail 176-198 mm, hindfoot 39-2-
Habitat. Probably forests and forest edges.
Movements, Home range and Social organization. No information.
Bibliography. Clayton (2016h), Corbet & Hill (1992), Miller (1903a), Molur et al. (2005), Musser (1986), Musser & Carleton (2005), Musser & Califia (1982), Musser & Heaney (1985).
